nutrient_source.title: Carbonated soft drink, flavoured, with added sugar — estimated from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy entry for carbonated beverage with sugar syrup, adjusted for purple sweet potato flavouring and honey content
nutrient_source.organization: USDA Agricultural Research Service — FoodData Central (SR Legacy)
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No direct entry exists for this specific Thai restaurant sparkling soda. Values estimated by analogy from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy data for sweetened carbonated soft drinks (FDC ID 174851, 'Carbonated beverage, cola, contains caffeine') and adjusted downward for lower sugar content typical of lightly sweetened Thai sodas with honey and sweet potato flavouring. Purple sweet potato contributes negligible macronutrients at typical flavouring concentrations. Honey pop candy (pop rocks type) contributes a small amount of sugar and trace fat. Protein and fat are effectively zero by formulation. Energy is driven entirely by carbohydrate (sugar) content.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2021 — sucrose and sugar-sweetened carbonated beverages
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / Atkinson FS et al., American Journal of Clinical Nutrition, 2021
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for this specific Thai sparkling soda. GI of 63 is estimated by analogy from the University of Sydney GI Database value for sucrose (GI 65) and lightly sweetened carbonated soft drinks (GI range 59–66), as the primary glycemic driver in this beverage is dissolved sugars (honey and sweet potato syrup). GL per serving calculated as: (GI × carbohydrate_g per serving) / 100 = (63 × 7.0 × 300/100) / 100 = 63 × 21.0 / 100 = 13.23, rounded to 13.1. gi_category set to medium (GI 56–69 range).

hypertension.key_factor: Very low sodium content (~15 mg per 300 g serving)
hypertension.guidance: This sparkling soda contains negligible sodium (~15 mg per serving) and poses minimal direct risk for hypertension; however, regular consumption of sugar-sweetened beverages may contribute to weight gain, which is an indirect hypertension risk factor.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Free sugar content from honey and sweet potato syrup with medium glycemic index (~63)
type2_diabetes.guidance: Individuals with Type 2 Diabetes should limit consumption of this beverage due to its free sugar content (~19.5 g per 300 g serving) and medium GI of approximately 63, which can cause a moderate postprandial blood glucose rise; occasional small servings may be tolerated within a carbohydrate-controlled meal plan.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Negligible fat and sodium; moderate free sugar intake
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: This beverage contains no fat or cholesterol and very low sodium, posing minimal direct cardiovascular risk per serving; however, habitual consumption of sugar-sweetened beverages is associated with elevated triglycerides and increased cardiovascular risk, so moderation is advised.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Violet Sweet Potato Honey Pop Sparkling Soda (Thai: โซดาเผือกม่วงน้ำผึ้งป๊อป) is a Thai-style flavoured sparkling beverage served at Saap Saap Thai restaurant in Singapore. It is made from carbonated soda water infused with purple sweet potato flavouring and sweetened with honey, then topped with honey-flavoured pop rocks candy that creates a distinctive crackling and popping sensation when consumed. The drink presents a vibrant purple colour from the anthocyanin-rich purple sweet potato extract. The flavour profile is lightly sweet with floral honey notes and a refreshing effervescence. The pop rocks topping adds a playful crunchy and fizzing textural element that distinguishes this beverage from standard sodas. Nutritionally, this is a lightly sweetened carbonated beverage with approximately 28 kcal per 100 g, driven entirely by dissolved sugars (approximately 7 g carbohydrate per 100 g). Protein and fat are effectively zero by formulation. The glycemic index is estimated at approximately 63 (medium), making it a moderate glycemic beverage. It is suitable for vegetarians and is generally halal-friendly. Individuals with Type 2 Diabetes should consume this beverage in moderation due to its free sugar content. The beverage is not suitable for vegans due to the lactose content in pop rocks candy. It is a novelty Thai dessert drink popular in Singapore's Thai casual dining segment.

confidence_reason: No direct nutritional entry exists in any approved primary source for this specific Thai restaurant sparkling soda. Nutrient values are estimated by analogy from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy data for sweetened carbonated soft drinks, adjusted for the specific ingredients described. GI is estimated by analogy from University of Sydney GI Database values for sucrose and sugar-sweetened carbonated beverages. Confidence is low due to the absence of a direct source match and the proprietary restaurant formulation.
notes: Protein_g is confirmed zero — contains no protein by formulation; this is a sugar-water-based carbonated beverage with no protein-contributing ingredients. Fat_g is confirmed zero — contains no fat by formulation; the only potential fat source (lactose in pop rocks) contributes negligible fat below detection threshold. Saturated_fat_g, monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, trans_fat_g, and cholesterol_mg are all confirmed zero by formulation. Dietary_fibre_g is confirmed zero — the purple sweet potato is used as a flavouring/colouring extract at concentrations too low to contribute measurable fibre. The food_name has been normalised from the menu-style 'SS3 Soda Violet Honey Pop' to the canonical English descriptor 'Violet Sweet Potato Honey Pop Sparkling Soda'; the original menu name is preserved in aliases. Cuisine tags 'Pad Thai' and 'Thai Noodle' from the input are not applicable to this beverage and have been dropped. This record originates from field survey data seed data (GD001580) served by Saap Saap Thai - Funan.
